content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF SUTTER
1160 Civic Center Boulevard
Yuba City, California 95993

DARRELL W. LARSEN
County Counsel

Telephone (916) 741-7110

RONALD S. ERICKSON
Assistant

JAMES SCANLON
JOSEPH P. CERULLO
DENNIS E. LE CLERE
Deputies

September 17, 1992

VIA FAX NO. (916) 327-2026

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE:

REQUEST FOR ADVICE

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

We are requesting this advice on behalf of Sutter County
Supervisors Peter Licari, Barbara LeVake, Ronald Southard, and
Larry Montna and Sutter County Planning Commissioner Bruce Capaul.

In December of 1991, the Sutter County Board of Supervisors adopted
a general plan amendment ("GPA") encompassing approximately 24,715
acres in the southern part of the county.

Subsequently, objectors to the GPA filed a referendum petition
seeking to have the Board of Supervisors rescind the GPA or place
the matter on the ballot for a vote by the people. The Board of
Supervisors did neither. As a consequence, the objectors filed a
petition for a writ of mandate in the Sutter County Superior Court
seeking an order requiring the Board to rescind the GPA or place it
on the ballot. [Protect Sutter County-Let Us Vote, et al. v. Sutter
County, et al., case number 49764] Commissioner Bruce Capaul is a
named petitioner in such lawsuit.

We anticipate that during the months of September, October, and
November various matters relating to the GPA will come before the
Sutter County Planning Commission for consideration. These matters
include a specific plan within the GPA area, a transfer of
development credits (TDC) program, tentative maps with the area,
rezonings, and review of environmental documents related to any of
the above.

We are requesting advice as to whether Commissioner Capaul may
participate and vote on these or other matters implementing the GPA
in view of the fact that he is a petitioner in an action against
the county. Capaul states that he has contributed not in
excess of $230 to Protect Sutter County-Let Us Vote ("PSC-LUV"), a
political action committee, and also a petitioner in the above-

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
September 17, 1992
Page Two


named lawsuit.  Commissioner Capaul presumes that some or all of
this amount was used for attorney's fees in the writ of mandate.
Mr. Capaul further states that no legal obligation
to contribute or donate additional funds to PSC-LUV in the
prosecution of the lawsuit.  In the writ of mandate, petitioners
pray for attorney's fees.  However, should petitioners prevail, we
are informed that any attorney's fees awarded would not accrue to
PSC-LUV's individual donors.

We would also note that Commissioner Capaul obtained preliminary
telephone assistance from D. M. Stone of the FPPC on September 9,
1992.

We make this request for advice pursuant to, and as contemplated
by, Government Code section 83114.  Because GPA related matters
will be coming before the Commission in a very short period of
time, your earliest reply would be greatly appreciated.

Very truly yours,

DARRELL W. LARSEN
SUTTER COUNTY COUNSEL

[signature]
RONALD S. ERICKSON
ASSISTANT COUNTY COUNSEL

RSE:js

cc:  Supervisor Larry Montna
     Supervisor Barbara LeVake
     Supervisor Ronald Southard
     Supervisor Peter Licari
     Commissioner Bruce Capaul
     Larry T. Combs, Administrative Officer

sections.question: We are requesting advice as to whether Commissioner Capaul may participate and vote on these or other matters implementing the GPA in view of the fact that he is a petitioner in an action against the county. Mr. Capaul states that he has contributed not in excess of $230 to Protect Sutter County-Let Us Vote ('PSC-LW'), a political action committee, and also a petitioner in the above-named lawsuit. Commissioner Capaul presumes that some or all of this amount was used for attorney's fees in the writ of mandate.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Planning Commissioner Bruce Capaul may participate and vote on matters implementing a general plan amendment when he is a named petitioner in a lawsuit against the county challenging that amendment and has contributed funds to the political action committee that is also a petitioner in the lawsuit.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section present. The question is clearly stated in the body of the letter. Request made pursuant to Government Code Section 83114.

Mr. Capaul states that he has contributed not in excess of $230 to Protect Sutter County-Let Us Vote ('PSC-LW'), a political action committee, and also a petitioner in the above-named lawsuit. Commissioner Capaul presumes that some or all of this amount was used for attorney's fees in the writ of mandate.

embedding.summary: Sutter County Counsel requests FPPC advice on whether Planning Commissioner Bruce Capaul may participate in Planning Commission decisions on matters implementing a contested general plan amendment, given that Capaul is a named petitioner in a lawsuit against the county over the amendment and has contributed approximately $230 to the political action committee leading the lawsuit.
